queue.name: ID10253: Theorem 3: Beat Frequency Mechanism: T_semi

master_row.title: Theorem 3: Beat Frequency Mechanism: T_semi
master_row.canonical_statement: The beat frequency between Earth's rotation and the lunar orbit is \[ \omega_{\text{beat}} = \omega_\oplus - \omega_{\text{Moon}}, \] yielding a semidiurnal tide period \[ T_{\text{semi}} = \frac{\pi}{\omega_{\text{beat}}} \approx 12.42~\mathrm{hours}, \] matching observations. Newton's framework cannot explain why tides are semidiurnal (two per day) rather than purely diurnal; Trawin Topology shows this arises from two counter-rotating wave systems beating together.
master_row.formation_note: Recovered from a D:\All source document under ‘Theorem 3: Beat Frequency Mechanism’.
master_row.dictionary: The beat frequency between Earth's rotation and the lunar orbit is yielding a semidiurnal tide period matching observations (T_semi)
master_row.encyclopedia: Theorem 3: Beat Frequency Mechanism: T_semi is a scaling / order-of-magnitude relation, written T_semi = fracπω_beat ≈ 12.42~hours. It expresses a scaling or proportionality, capturing how the quantity grows with its parameters. It is built from π, defining T_semi. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
